commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Inconsistency between defined hook and usage: hooks.ts defines 'useComponent' taking 'Props', but components/index.tsx attempts to import 'useWeather' with a different signature.
issues.fix: Rename useComponent to useWeather in hooks.ts and ensure the signature matches the parameters (temp, wind, humidity, unit) used in the component file.

issues.description: Weather icons lack descriptive aria-labels for screen readers.
issues.fix: Add aria-label={condition} to the Icon component in CurrentWeather and aria-hidden="true" to decorative emojis.

issues.description: The forecast strip uses overflowX: 'auto' but doesn't define a minimum width for forecast items, which may cause squishing on very small screens.
issues.fix: Add a minWidth (e.g., 60px) to the ForecastItem container.

evaluations.critique: The component is visually excellent, following design system tokens perfectly and creating a professional weather dashboard feel. While the UI structure is robust, there is a technical mismatch in how the hooks are imported and called between the entrypoint and the sub-components.
